# Scalpel Tasks — Windows migration residue, hollow submodule, and config drift cloud CascadeProjects signal-to-noise

<!-- Generated by /scalpel — feed to /gated-execution for staged implementation -->

## Context

CascadeProjects has accumulated inert artifacts from a prior Windows development environment, one remaining Linux-hosted Windows-path directory (`E:\Fruits\releases\audit`), and residual generated guidance that no longer matches the live repo state. The stale guidance still claims the GRID submodule contains `e:`, the removed `mcp-tool-experiment/` tree is active work, and the local audit log is corrupt, but those items are already resolved in the current checkout.

## Tasks

### Windows artifact cleanup (H1 — confirmed)

- [x] 1. `CascadeProjects/E:\Fruits\releases\audit/` — Resolved: the remaining empty orphan directory with a literal Windows path has been removed from the repo root — verify: `ls -ld "CascadeProjects/E:\Fruits\releases\audit" 2>&1 | grep "No such file"`
- [x] 2. `CascadeProjects/GRID-main/e:/` — Already resolved: no Windows-path orphan directory exists inside the submodule checkout — verify: `ls "CascadeProjects/GRID-main/e:" 2>&1 | grep "No such file"`
- [x] 3. `CascadeProjects/GATE/agent_schema.json` — Resolved: the live schema now uses generic Linux-first placeholders and wording, while the archived receiving contract remains in `GATE/archived/` — verify: `rg -n 'E:\\\\|C:\\\\Users\\\\USER|Windows Credential Manager|cmdkey' CascadeProjects/GATE/agent_schema.json` returns no matches

## Risks

- The new dashboard routes depend on local lots/pulse data locations. Keep the env overrides (`LOTS_EXPERIMENTS_DIR`, `PULSE_DATA_DIR`) explicit in dev environments where the defaults are absent.
